text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like functions or methods, ensuring they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of the system work together smoothly. Test-driven development (TDD) is a method where you write a test before writing the actual code. This practice encourages careful planning and cleaner code. Overall, testing boosts confidence in the software. When a project has a strong test suite, developers can make changes or add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality. This saves time and reduces errors in the long run.
